propositions: Add an IntrospectionTokenVerifier that POSTs opaque access tokens to an RFC 7662 /introspect endpoint with client_secret_basic auth and maps active:true responses into an AccessToken.

Register flat auth_introspection_* config keys in McpConfig/_SECTIONS['mcp'] and derive REBAR_MCP_AUTH_INTROSPECTION_* env-var names into MCP_ENV_VARS and server.json so the manifest drift check passes.

Map RFC 7662 optional response fields with defined fallbacks: scope split into scopes, exp to expires_at (absent to None, never 0), sub to subject, client_id falling back to sub then 'unknown', stamping resource=auth_resource_server_url.
Enforce fail-closed behavior: HTTPS-only endpoint, bounded timeouts, rejection of private/link-local hosts by default, and raising on non-200, timeout, or malformed responses so the composite denies.
Validate audience per §2.2 accepting aud as string or array, rejecting mismatch or absent aud by default unless auth_introspection_allow_missing_aud is true.
Refuse to start when the env var named by auth_introspection_client_secret_env is absent or empty, and never cache or log tokens/secrets so every request re-introspects for instant revocation.
